tool.name: vg deconstruct
tool.description: VCF projection of a variation graph against a reference path
tool.command:
#if str($graph.ext) in ('gfa1', 'gfa1.gz') ## vg deconstruct cannot read GFA directly; convert to vg first #if str($graph.ext) == 'gfa1.gz' gunzip -c '$graph' > input.gfa && #else ln -s '$graph' input.gfa && #end if vg convert -g -p input.gfa > input.vg && #set $graph_arg = 'input.vg' #else ln -s '$graph' 'input.${graph.ext}' && #set $graph_arg = 'input.' + str($graph.ext) #end if
vg deconstruct --threads \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1} #if str($ref_mode.mode) == "path" #for $p in str($ref_mode.path).strip().split(',') --path '$p.strip()' #end for #else #for $p in str($ref_mode.path_prefix).strip().split(',') --path-prefix '$p.strip()' #end for #end if #if str($alt_prefix): --alt-prefix '${alt_prefix}' #end if #if $snarls: --snarls '$snarls' #end if $path_traversals $all_snarls $contig_only_ref '$graph_arg' > '$output_vcf'

param.label: Input graph
param.help: Variation graph in any vg-readable format (vg/xg/GFA). PanSN-named paths recommended for prefix mode.

param.label: Reference path names
param.help: Comma-separated path names to deconstruct against, e.g. 'chr1,chr2'.

param.label: Reference path prefix(es)
param.help: Comma-separated PanSN prefixes. All paths starting with this prefix are treated as reference.

param.label: Non-reference path prefix(es)
param.help: Paths beginning with this value get lumped together to the same sample in VCF. Comma-separated list accepted.

param.label: Only consider traversals that correspond to paths in the graph (-e)
Report all nested snarls (-a)
param.help: By default only top-level snarls are reported.

tool.help:
**vg deconstruct** — project a variation graph to VCF.
Walks the snarls of a variation graph relative to a reference path (or PanSN-prefix path set) and emits a VCF describing each variant.
Standard pangenome VCF projection: for an 8-strain pangenome with PanSN naming `SAMPLE#1#CHR`, set path prefix to `GCA_900093555.2` to use the PvP01 reference and get an 8-sample VCF in PvP01 coordinates.
-----
**Modes**
- `-p` (path): explicit reference path name(s). - `-P` (path-prefix): all paths whose name begins with the prefix are used as reference — the canonical PanSN approach.
**Additional options**
- `--alt-prefix`: non-reference paths beginning with this prefix get lumped together to the same sample in VCF. - `--snarls`: provide a pre-computed snarl file to avoid recomputation. - `-e` (--path-traversals): only consider traversals that correspond to paths in the graph. - `-a` (--all-snarls): report all nested snarls (default: top-level only). - `-C` (--contig-only-ref): use CONTIG-only reference names.
Threads from `GALAXY_SLOTS`.
